defs: One who, or that which, makes flat or flattens.
A drawplate with a narrow, rectangular orifice, for drawing flat strips, as watch springs, etc.

records.ety: [OE. flateren, cf. OD. flatteren; akin to G. flattern to flutter, Icel. flaðra to fawn, flatter: cf. F. flatter. Cf. Flitter, Flutter, Flattery.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Flattered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Flattering.]

defs: To treat with praise or blandishments; to gratify or attempt to gratify the self-love or vanity of, esp. by artful and interested commendation or attentions; to blandish; to cajole; to wheedle.
quotes: When I tell him he hates flatterers, He says he does, being then most flattered.
A man that flattereth his neighbor, spreadeth a net for his feet.
Others he flattered by asking their advice.

defs: To raise hopes in; to encourage or favorable, but sometimes unfounded or deceitful, representations.
To portray too favorably; to give a too favorable idea of; as, his portrait flatters him.
To use flattery or insincere praise.
quotes: If it may stand him more in stead to lie, Say and unsay, feign, flatter, or adjure.
